title: Reasoning, Planning, Decomposition, and Verification · Evaluation Brief: Reasoning Selection
summary: Prompt a difficult migration without exposing or demanding hidden reasoning. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt reasoning selection increment in a plan-execute-verify task contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure.
Version fixed prompt text separately from variable inputs and evaluation data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Predict Reasoning Selection
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Reasoning Selection contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure.

content.text: Request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Reasoning Selection, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a plan-execute-verify task contract.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Reliable reasoning requires the model to print every private thought. This fails the Reasoning Selection contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Anthropic Agent Evaluation Guidance (2026) — Controls multi-turn and tool-using evaluation design for agentic prompts.

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Reasoning Selection, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Build Reasoning Selection evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-reasoning-selection-1lg27v" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Reasoning Selection into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-reasoning-selection-1lg27v
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

steps.title: Defend the Reasoning Selection model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Reasoning Selection applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure.
